recentArticles.title: They Don’t Make Celebrities Like Michael Jackson Anymore - The Atlantic
recentArticles.snippet: The success of *Michael* suggests that audiences are nostalgic for a universal kind of fame that’s rare today. In the 1980s, the former Los Angeles Lakers superstar invited Jackson to a Lakers game, an invitation the singer was initially hesitant to accept because he was worried that his presence would create too much of a frenzy. He existed on a truly singular plane of stardom—and nearly 20 years after his death, he still inspires a unique level of obsession, devotion, and curiosity from fans, even those who weren’t alive to see him in the flesh. The enormous success of *Michael*, the recently released biopic about Jackson’s life, is a testament to that staying power. As I watched *Michael* in the theater, I was flooded by my own memories of Jackson.
recentArticles.summary: 此新聞內容主要探討麥可傑克森的普遍性名氣與當代名人現象，與周杰倫無直接關聯。

recentArticles.title: Jung Kook Collaborates With Calvin Klein on a Limited-edition Capsule - WWD
recentArticles.snippet: # EXCLUSIVE: Calvin Klein Partners With Jung Kook on Limited-edition Capsule, Blending Iconic Designs and Rebellious Style. The 20-style capsule reimagines the brand's essentials with Jung Kook's edgy, biker-inspired aesthetic. * Share this article on Talk. Calvin Klein Inc., part of PVH Corp., will launch Jung Kook for Calvin Klein, a limited-edition men’s and women’s capsule in partnership with global brand ambassador Jung Kook, marking his first fashion collaboration. Building on the brand’s long-standing ambassadorship with the artist, the men’s and women’s 20-style capsule reimagines Calvin Klein’s essentials through the lens of Jung Kook’s rebellious and edgy style, blending biker attitude with Calvin Klein’s storied design codes. Discussing why he thinks a Jung Kook by Calvin Klein capsule will resonate with his customers, and whether he believes it will attract new customers to the brand, Savman told WWD, “Jung Kook has been a global ambassador for Calvin Klein for years, and we’ve seen the powerful connection he has with his audience globally.

recentArticles.title: Drake surprise drops three albums and mentions rapper feud - BBC
recentArticles.snippet: # Drake surprise drops three albums and mentions the Kendrick Lamar beef. The rapper's dropped his anticipated solo record, Iceman, plus two more - Habibti and Maid of Honour - with 43 songs in total. They arethe first albums the Canadian has dropped since his beef with US rapper Kendrick Lamar escalated in 2024. And, yes, he does bring it up on the new record. The big question about the heavily hyped new release was whether Drake would use his new music to reignite his bitter feud with Lamar. And on Iceman album opener, Make Them Cry, it looks like Drake has given a glimpse into his feelings at the height of the rap beef. Most critics have yet to share reviews of Iceman and Drake's two other new releases, but reports suggest that streaming services were hit with crashes and slowdowns as fans rushed to hear his new music. Drake has been building excitement for his new album with a series of Iceman livestreams.
recentArticles.summary: Drake surprise drops three albums and mentions rapper feud - BBC

recentArticles.title: Waymo service in Atlanta still suspended after self-driving car stuck in Midtown floodwaters - Atlanta News First
recentArticles.snippet: # Waymo service in Atlanta still suspended after self-driving car stuck in Midtown floodwaters. (Atlanta News First) — Waymo’s self-driving ride-hailing service in Atlanta remained suspended after one of the company’s autonomous vehicles became stuck in floodwaters on a Midtown street Wednesday night. No one was inside the car at the time, according to Waymo. Georgia Tech researchers said the incident highlights a known challenge for autonomous vehicles: judging the depth of water on flooded streets. That can be really a problem for flooding,” said Georgia Tech professor Glen Chou on LIDAR and radar. Chou described the situation as a relatively small issue that can be addressed as autonomous vehicle systems continue to collect data. Dr. Srinivas Peeta, who leads the Autonomous and Connected Transportation Lab at Georgia Tech, said incidents like this can be surprising for the public. Peeta said the vehicles are safe under normal conditions and that safety should improve over time as the technology advances.

recentArticles.title: Lieutenant Governor Jay Collins Visits Northbridge University to Highlight Workforce Training and Career Education in Florida - Positively Osceola
recentArticles.snippet: # Lieutenant Governor Jay Collins Visits Northbridge University to Highlight Workforce Training and Career Education in Florida. — Florida Lieutenant Governor Jay Collins visited Northbridge University on Wednesday afternoon for a workforce development roundtable and campus tour focused on strengthening Florida’s skilled labor pipeline and expanding career-focused education opportunities. The visit brought together university leadership, elected officials, economic development leaders, business executives, and community stakeholders for discussions centered on workforce readiness, healthcare education, skilled trades training, and employer-driven academic pathways designed to meet Florida’s growing labor demands. **“We were honored to welcome Lieutenant Governor Collins to our Kissimmee campus for a meaningful conversation about the future of higher education and workforce development in Florida,” said Dr. James Michael Burkett, president of U.S. operations for Northbridge University. — El vicegobernador de Florida, Jay Collins, visitó el miércoles el campus de Northbridge University para participar en una mesa redonda sobre desarrollo laboral y realizar un recorrido por las instalaciones enfocadas en la capacitación de la futura fuerza laboral del estado.

recentArticles.title: Harry Styles Team Addresses Fan Complaints About Obstructed Views - Stereogum
recentArticles.snippet: Following a special one-off Manchester gig in March, Harry Styles has officially commenced his tour in support of his recent album *Kiss All The Time. So far he's played two nights in Amsterdam, with setlists that include almost all of his new songs, a decent selection of his old songs, and little nods to Talking Heads, Underworld, and Paul Simon. But the setlist isn't what's been getting people talking about Styles' tour; his stage setup is. That's no problem if you're in the stadium seats, but for fans who paid hundreds of euros for GA tickets and spent hours queuing outside the venue, they're being met with some pretty severely obstructed views at certain points of the set. "The floor concept was designed to give fans freedom of movement and the ability to experience the show from different positions, rather than being confined to one fixed viewing angle," a rep told TMZ.

Todd Martens is a features columnist at the Los Angeles Times who writes about theme parks and West Coast Experiences, among other topics. His weekly newsletter, Mr. Todd’s Wild Ride, covers the ever-changing world of theme parks from SoCal. Martens joined the Los Angeles Times in 2007 and has covered a mix of interactive entertainment as its game critic and pop music as a reporter and editor. recentArticles.snippet: # Early 2010s pop star teasing a San Francisco move returns for another Bay Area concert. Weeks after telling a San Francisco crowd she planned to move to the city, Kesha is heading back to the Bay Area for another performance. The “TiKToK” singer, who performed a pop-up DJ set at the Presidio earlier this month, is scheduled to headline a concert at the Toyota Pavilion at Concord on Wednesday, May 27, as part of her recently-launched Freedom Tour. Through her own label, Kesha Records, the singer has leaned back into the maximalist pop sound that made her a star while embracing a freer, more experimental style. She joined the Chronicle as a summer 2023 intern for the Datebook team. She previously interned for the San Diego Union-Tribune and wrote for her campus newspaper, the Guardian, where she served as editor-in-chief. recentArticles.snippet: Local News |   Community news: Jay It Forward Giving Day…. # Community news: Jay It Forward Giving Day raises over $300K, and more. Jay It Forward Giving Day 2026 at Elmhurst University raised $313,137 during the 24-hour fundraiser, thanks to more than 500 alums, friends and members of the campus community. “Jay It Forward Giving Day demonstrates the strength of the Elmhurst University community and our shared commitment to advancing the University’s future,” Lia Kampman, vice president for Institutional Advancement shared via a news release. The money will go toward athletics for EU’s 21 Division III teams for transportation, training equipment and other needs; the Learning and Success Academy, a post-secondary certificate program for young adults with differing abilities celebrating its 20th anniversary, for textbooks, digital learning tools and other resources; The Annual Fund, which provides student scholarships,program and operational costs and other university needs; and Choose Your Way to Jay It Forward, which supports university programs chosen by the donor, such as the Mill Theatre, Mock Trial program, academic areas, and Bluejay Nest Fund for student emergencies.

recentArticles.title: Celtics in no rush to give Jaylen Brown extension? - Yahoo Sports
recentArticles.snippet: * The Kevin O'Connor Show. ### Indoor Football League: Vegas Knight Hawks-Orlando Pirates. # Celtics in no rush to give Jaylen Brown extension? > Brad Stevens is no stranger to extensions but giving out that type of money to a 33-year-old is far from a no brainer. Adding that type of long-term commitment is more likely to hurt than help Brown’s trade value in the present even after his standout regular season in 2025-26. Quite simply, there’s no sense of urgency here from a Celtics standpoint to do an extension now beyond just appeasing Brown. That’s not going to be the impetus for Stevens’ next big deal. He’s got bigger decisions to make for the present and making a decision on Brown’s long-term future doesn’t help him on that front. For that reason, don’t expect an extension to be among Stevens’ priorities this summer. *This article originally appeared on Hoops Hype: Celtics in no rush to give Jaylen Brown extension?*.

recentArticles.title: Paris Jackson Asking For Extra $463K in Battle With Late Dad’s Estate - TMZ
recentArticles.snippet: Last week, a Los Angeles judge sided with Paris when he ruled that $625K in bonuses paid to outside law firms that worked on the estate would have to be returned to the estate. **John Branca** and **John McClain**, the two executors in charge of **Michael Jackson**’s estate since his death in 2009, argued the bonuses paid to the third-party law firms were normal for the music industry ... At the time, the executors’ rep told TMZ, "We are gratified that the Court itself recognized and praised the work of the Executors and its outside counsel in today's decision.". The rep added, “While the Court has previously approved several other bonuses to outside counsel over the years for their extraordinary services, and this was the first time they were the subject of objections, the Executors have always understood that legal fees are subject to court approval and have always required outside counsel to agree to return any funds to the estate if the payments were not approved.”.

recentArticles.title: Paris Jackson scores $625K legal win against lawyers, gains more control over family assets - New York Post
recentArticles.snippet: The court sustained Paris’ objection to the bonuses paid by executors John Branca and John McClain — who she claims are abusing their positions — to attorneys for the Jackson estate during the second half of 2018, and the judge ruled Paris can seek reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs. “Ms. Jackson’s objection to the $625,000 of bonus payments made in the second six months of 2018 is sustained. After months of engaging in sexist, scorched-earth tactics against a beneficiary, it’s time for John Branca to acknowledge his many missteps and act in the best interest of the family he has a fiduciary duty to protect.”. “And, to be clear, none of the $625,000 in bonuses – which represent only a small fraction of the Estate’s expenses for the period in question —  were paid to the executors, and the court did not in any way say that the executors had made any inappropriate payments to themselves,” the statement continues.

recentArticles.title: Harry Styles Makes a Triumphant Comeback in Amsterdam Tour Opener - Rolling Stone
recentArticles.snippet: “I got older and the stage got bigger,” singer says as his Together, Together Tour kicks off in Amsterdam. It’s an absolute pleasure to be here with you this evening. This is Night One of the Together, Together Tour.” Harry Styles is back, all right. Harry celebrated the start of his massive new Together, Together run with a crowd of 56,000 frenzied fans. If anyone needed proof, one of the countless peaks of this show: “Treat People With Kindness,” a song nobody saw coming, but one that exploded into a jubilant sing-along — the anthem that made the fans on the floor throw their bags and coats in a pile so they could join hands and dance in a witchy circle around it.
recentArticles.summary: 哈利·史泰爾斯在阿姆斯特丹成功開啟了他的「Together, Together」巡演，現場氣氛熱烈，粉絲反應狂熱。

recentArticles.title: Michael Jackson Holds U.K. Albums Chart Crown as Compilation Scores Second Week at No. 1 - Billboard
recentArticles.snippet: # Michael Jackson Holds U.K. Albums Chart Crown as Compilation Scores Second Week at No. 1. The singer's return to the top spot on the chart follows the release of the blockbuster biopic. Michael Jackson continues his renewed dominance on the U.K. charts, as *The Essential Michael Jackson* spends a second consecutive week at No. 1 on the Official Albums Chart dated May 15. The hit-packed release becomes the Irish boyband’s 15th top 10 LP in the U.K. Renewed interest in Jackson’s catalogue follows the blockbuster *Michael* biopic, which has fueled strong streaming and physical sales across the singer’s discography in recent weeks. *The Essential* returned to No. 1 for the first time in 17 years on the previous chart frame (May 8) and now extends its reign atop the summit. The record becomes the band’s third U.K. Top 10 entry, following 2007 breakthrough *The State of Things* and 2023 release *Heatwave in the Cold North*. ### Get in the know on.

recentArticles.title: Drake Is Back With Three New Albums: 'Iceman,' ‘Maid of Honour’ & ‘Habibti’ - Billboard
recentArticles.snippet: # Drake Is Back With Three New Albums: ‘Iceman,’ ‘Maid of Honour’ & ‘Habibti’ | Billboard News. Drake returns by releasing three new albums ‘Iceman,’ ‘Maid of Honour’ and ‘Habibti.’ There are references to BTS and shots at DJ Khaled and LeBron James, and he reignites his feud with Kendrick Lamar through a new collaboration with Future on “Run to Atlanta” from ‘Iceman’ and more! So Drake just released a lot of new music, and besides name-dropping global superstars BTS on the track “Make Them Cry” that has left the fellas and their fans maybe a little confused, he also decided to bring 2024 back and his beef with K-Dot. He dropped a track that features Future, a frequent Drake collaborator who was also on a Kendrick diss track, and of course the title “Ran to Atlanta” references the famous “Not Like Us” lyric.

recentArticles.title: Jay Fortune's late goal helps Atlanta United earn 1-1 draw with Orlando City - The Washington Post
recentArticles.snippet: # Jay Fortune's late goal helps Atlanta United earn 1-1 draw with Orlando City. Jay Fortune scored late in the second half to rally Atlanta United to a 1-1 draw with Orlando City. — Jay Fortune scored late in the second half to rally Atlanta United to a 1-1 draw with Orlando City on Saturday night. Fortune used assists from defenders Matthew Edwards and Stian Gregersen in the 86th minute to score for the second time this season and the fourth time in 64 career appearances. Both defenders checked into the match in the 68th minute before notching their first assists this season. Subscribe for unlimited access to The Post. recentArticles.title: The hottest party in NYC this week was the premiere of a new YouTube show from the 'Subway Takes' creator - Business Insider
recentArticles.snippet: # The hottest party in NYC this week was the premiere of a new YouTube show from the 'Subway Takes' creator. * He premiered it at a theater in New York City. * Let me take you inside the creator-studded night, complete with taxi cabs and a swanky after-party. This week, I headed to a packed movie theater in downtown New York City for the world premiere of "Keep the Meter Running," one of several shows YouTube announced at its star-studded Upfronts presentation. You go home, you sit on your couch, you open the YouTube app, and you watch this television show the same way that you would any other television show," Kareem Rahma, the creator and host of "Keep the Meter Running," told the packed theater. "If this is a web series, then so is every show on Netflix and every show on HBO Max because it's all the internet now," said Adam Faze, who produced the show alongside Rahma.
recentArticles.summary: 此新聞報導與周杰倫無關，主要講述YouTube新節目《Keep the Meter Running》在紐約的首映會。
